name: El Cerrado Trail
name_original: Parque Natural Montes de Málaga – circular por el Sendero El Cerrado
description: Hike the moderate 1.9-mile El Cerrado Trail in Parque Natural Montes de Málaga for panoramic views of Malaga and the coast.
region_intro: Hiking in Montes De Málaga offers access to a diverse natural landscape, primarily within the Montes de Málaga Natural Park. This region is characterized by a rugged, uneven massif featuring pine-topped hills, small valleys, and numerous riverbeds, including the Guadalmedina river. The landscape is dominated by extensive Aleppo pine forests, with areas also containing native holm oaks, cork oaks, and various riparian species along watercourses. The park provides an accessible escape into nature, just a short distance from Málaga city. Best easy hiking trails in Montes De Málaga The most popular easy hiking route is Ciudad Jardín - Puerto de la Torre Trail (PR-A 114), a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This path offers a straightforward walk through the local terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pocopan Trail – Montes de Malaga Natural Park, an easy 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This route is known for its arboreal interest and offers views over a dense pine forest. Local hikers also love the Pocopán Viewpoint loop from Parque Natural Montes de Málaga, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Hiking in Montes De Málaga is defined by pine-topped hills, small valleys, and diverse forest landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, with a strong focus on easy hiking trails. The routes in Montes De Málaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1000 reviews. More than 10000 hikers have used komoot to explore Montes De Málaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are there in Montes de Málaga?
A: Montes de Málaga offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 160 routes available. These trails are designed to be accessible and enjoyable for various fitness levels, primarily within the Montes de Málaga Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy circular hiking routes in Montes de Málaga?
A: Yes, Montes de Málaga features several easy circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5288074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pocopán Viewpoint loop from Parque Natural Montes de Málaga&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.6 km long and takes about 1 hour 38 minutes. The El Cerrado Trail, mentioned in regional information, is also a circular route offering impressive views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on easy hikes in Montes de Málaga?
A: While the Montes de Málaga Natural Park is generally welcoming to visitors, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific regulations for pets within the park should be checked locally, but many trails are suitable for leashed dogs.

Q: Are there easy trails suitable for families with children?
A: Absolutely. Many easy trails in Montes de Málaga are ideal for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995564261/pocopan-trail-montes-de-malaga-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pocopan Trail – Montes de Malaga Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is an easy 5.1 km path known for its arboreal interest and gentle terrain, making it suitable for a family outing. The Torrijos Trail, starting at the eco-museum, is also a short and easy option.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on easy hikes?
A: Easy hikes in Montes de Málaga will immerse you in a diverse natural landscape. You'll primarily encounter magnificent Aleppo pine forests, along with areas of native holm oaks, cork oaks, and various riparian species along watercourses. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Málaga Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is characterized by pine-topped hills, small valleys, and numerous riverbeds. The Picapedreros-Boticario Trail, for instance, passes by the Picapedreros waterfall.

Q: Are there viewpoints accessible on easy trails in Montes de Málaga?
A: Yes, several easy trails lead to or offer access to stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5288074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pocopán Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; provides incredible vistas over dense pine forests. Other notable viewpoints in the park include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/132339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1006359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Francisco Vázquez Sell Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which on clear days can offer glimpses of the African coast.

Q: How can I get to the easy hiking trails using public transport?
A: Montes de Málaga Natural Park is conveniently located just five kilometers north of Málaga city, making it relatively accessible. While specific bus routes directly to trailheads might vary, public transport options from Málaga city can get you close to the park's entrances. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes heading towards the Montes de Málaga area.

Q: Where can I park for easy hikes in Montes de Málaga?
A: Parking is generally available at various access points and visitor centers within the Montes de Málaga Natural Park. For example, the Aula de Naturaleza Las Contadores, located in the center of the park, serves as a starting point for trails like the Contadores Trail and likely offers parking facilities. The old Torrijos winery, now an eco-museum, also provides a starting point for the Torrijos Trail and has parking.

Q: What is the best time of year to go on easy hikes in Montes de Málaga?
A: The best time for easy hikes in Montes de Málaga is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and while winters are generally mild, some trails might be wet after rain.

Q: What are some interesting places to see near the easy hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, Montes de Málaga offers several points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/839247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen's Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, a historic man-made monument. The Torrijos Trail leads to the old Torrijos winery, now an eco-museum, offering insights into 19th-century life and the region's wine and olive oil production history. The park also contains ancient Prehistoric necropolises, adding a cultural dimension to your visit.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Montes de Málaga?
A: The easy trails in Montes de Málaga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1000 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet woodlands, the accessibility of the trails, and the beautiful panoramic views from various viewpoints. The diverse forest landscapes and well-maintained paths are frequently highlighted as positive aspects of the hiking experience.

Q: How long do easy hikes in Montes de Málaga typically take?
A: Easy hikes in Montes de Málaga vary in length and duration, but many can be completed within a few hours. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995557178/ciudad-jardin-puerto-de-la-torre-trail-pr-a-114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad Jardín - Puerto de la Torre Trail (PR-A 114)&lt;/a&gt; is 4.8 km and takes about 1 hour 20 minutes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995564261/pocopan-trail-montes-de-malaga-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pocopan Trail&lt;/a&gt; is 5.1 km and typically takes around 1 hour 25 minutes, making them perfect for a leisurely outing.
tips: Beautiful panoramic views of the entire Montes de Málaga Natural Park. Turning around the El Cerrado trail, it is an ideal itinerary to do with the whole family.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the El Cerrado Trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 1 hour and 10 minutes to complete. However, allow extra time to enjoy the panoramic views, especially from the Boar Viewpoint.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail features some narrow and steep stretches, characteristic of the mountainous landscape within Parque Natural Montes de Málaga. Expect a mix of paths that offer both challenging sections and rewarding vistas.

faqs.question: Is the El Cerrado Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While generally considered a moderate hike, its narrow and steep sections might be challenging for absolute beginners. Those with some hiking experience will find it enjoyable and manageable.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail, officially known as SL-A 137 Inicio Sendero 'El Cerrado', is located near Malaga. Specific parking details are best checked locally, but it's typically accessible within Parque Natural Montes de Málaga.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary draw of this trail is the breathtaking panoramic views of Malaga and the surrounding landscape. On clear days, you might even catch a glimpse of Gibraltar. Be sure to stop at the Boar Viewpoint for exceptional photo opportunities.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while hiking the El Cerrado Trail, you will also traverse sections of the official trails known as Sendero El Cerrado, [CIMA ML10] El León * Málaga, and Camino Bajo del Cerrado.

faqs.question: Is the El Cerrado Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific trail is not explicitly stated. When hiking in natural parks like Parque Natural Montes de Málaga, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the trail or Parque Natural Montes de Málaga?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the El Cerrado Trail or the Parque Natural Montes de Málaga itself for day hikes. However, always check the latest regulations from local authorities before your visit, as rules can change.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike in this region is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May), to avoid the intense summer heat. Spring also offers the chance to see wildflowers.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail offers opportunities for birdwatching, as various bird species inhabit the Parque Natural Montes de Málaga. Keep an eye out for local fauna, though larger animals are less commonly sighted during daytime hikes.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within Parque Natural Montes de Málaga, amenities directly on the trail are limited. It's recommended to bring your own water and snacks. You'll find cafes and restaurants in the nearby towns and Malaga city.
